title: Arrays, Slices, Backing Storage, Append, Copy, and Capacity
description: A batch processor must window records, grow buffers, preserve caller data, and avoid retention or aliasing defects. Learners cumulatively produce an alias-safe batch and buffer toolkit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use arrays as fixed-length value types and explain how length participates in type identity and copying.
Reason about slice length, capacity, backing storage, nil slices, empty non-nil slices, and observable API differences.
Use two-index and full slice expressions while proving bounds, capacity, and append authority.
